The existing schema, registry, seed data, server logic, and specs all matched each other, and the README scenario grammar was correct. The only gap was the missing `widget_id` field on Category, which is exactly what this task added.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No. The provided spec/context files were accurate and consistent. The specs matched the implementation, the schema/registry/seed/server files were internally coherent, and the server already contained the `type_detail` error envelope branch — it just wasn't activated until the `errorEnvelopeStyle` constant was switched. The only minor note is the promotions spec's Purpose placeholder ("TBD - created by archiving..."), but that's an incompleteness, not an inaccuracy.
tasks.nl_request: We're revising how the API communicates certain details to clients — bring it up to date.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No, nothing was inaccurate. The spec files, code, and task description were all consistent — the workspace correctly reflected Promotion as an existing feature (entity schema, routes, seed data, spec scenarios) that needed full removal. The only minor cosmetic note is that `openspec/specs/promotions/spec.md` had a placeholder Purpose ("TBD - created by archiving change add-promotion-list. Update Purpose after archive."), but that didn't affect the work.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The `server.ts` header comment claims "this file never changes across substrate configs" and that "Behavior is entirely data-driven from registry.ts + schema.ts + seed.ts." This is inaccurate for the partial-update feature: the data files alone cannot express merge-vs-replace semantics, so implementing PATCH partial updates required modifying `server.ts` itself (the update handler's merge logic).
tasks.nl_request: Updating a Widget should follow the newer partial-update convention the API now uses.
